Active facts:
- Intermittent fasting for one month was associated with increased evening snacking for this individual.
- At a farmers' market demo I attended, people asked more about plant proteins than about meat alternatives.
- My teenage cousin now brings his own lunch to school because of online food influencers—surprising for his age.
- Visually appealing but less healthy TikTok recipes go viral, which can normalize indulgent options and shape what people see as trendy to eat.
- Late-night delivery options are widespread.
- Snack subscription boxes make it easy to try new packaged items and can increase both variety and frequency of snacking.
- A friend signed up for a snack subscription box and is always trying novel packaged snacks these days.
- In a small workplace poll I ran, nearly half said they skipped breakfast at least some days.
- A family member (aunt) changed her soda habits after a health scare, leading the family to rethink sugary drinks.
- A Saturday community garden is popular with younger residents and provides small-scale food access.
- An economist on a podcast said food price swings are making people cook at home more often.
